title: mentha arvensis subsp. lanceolata
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. lanceolata, also known as Japanese wild mint, is a subspecies of the peppermint plant that is native to East Asia. It is a perennial herb with a distinctive flavor and aroma, commonly used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Japanese wild mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve mental clarity. Japanese wild mint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed fresh or dried to harness its medicinal properties.
